name: De novo pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis
summary: Ring-first pathway that builds the pyrimidine base from glutamine, bicarbonate and aspartate, attaches ribose-5-phosphate as PRPP to yield UMP, then phosphorylates and aminates UMP to UDP, UTP and CTP.
description: Unlike purine synthesis (which assembles the ring on ribose), the pyrimidine ring is built first and only later joined to ribose 5-phosphate. Six enzymatic activities carry orotate synthesis and UMP formation, concentrated in just three polypeptides. The first three steps are catalyzed by **CAD**, a single cytosolic trifunctional protein (carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase II, aspartate transcarbamoylase, dihydroorotase): glutamine + 2 ATP + bicarbonate give carbamoyl phosphate, which condenses with aspartate and cyclodehydrates to (S)-dihydroorotate. The fourth step is the pathway's only membrane/organellar reaction: **dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH)**, anchored in the inner mitochondrial membrane, oxidizes dihydroorotate to orotate and passes the electrons to ubiquinone of the respiratory chain — the molecular basis of DHODH-targeted drugs (leflunomide/teriflunomide, brequinar) and the reason proliferating cells couple pyrimidine synthesis to mitochondrial respiration. Orotate is then joined to PRPP and decarboxylated by the bifunctional **UMP synthase (UMPS)** to give UMP, the parent pyrimidine ribonucleotide. UMP is phosphorylated to UDP (UMP-CMP kinase) and UTP (nucleoside-diphosphate kinase), and UTP is aminated by **CTP synthase** to CTP. Flux is governed chiefly at CPS II (the CAD complex): the end product UTP is a feedback inhibitor, while PRPP and ATP are activators, and growth-factor signaling tunes these responses through MAPK (activating) and PKA (opposing) phosphorylation. The CTP branch is separately controlled by CTP feedback inhibition and GTP activation. Loss-of-function of UMPS causes hereditary orotic aciduria. (The classic allosteric ATCase inhibited by CTP is the E. coli paradigm; in mammals the ATCase activity is embedded in CAD and is not the primary control point.)

references.title: Regulation of carbamoyl phosphate synthetase by MAP kinase
Pyrimidine biosynthetic enzyme CAD: its function, regulation, and diagnostic potential
Regulation of the mammalian carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase II by effectors and phosphorylation (Shaw & Carrey, Eur. J. Biochem.)

compartments.name: Cytosol
compartments.description: Site of the CAD reactions (steps 1–3), UMP synthase (steps 5–6) and the UMP→UDP→UTP→CTP conversions (steps 7–9).

compartments.name: Mitochondrial inner membrane (outer face)
compartments.description: Dihydroorotate dehydrogenase is anchored here and delivers electrons to the ubiquinone pool of the respiratory chain (step 4).

enzymes.name: Multifunctional protein CAD (CPS II · aspartate transcarbamoylase · dihydroorotase)
enzymes.description: Single ~243 kDa cytosolic polypeptide that catalyzes the first three steps of pyrimidine synthesis; the CPS II domain is the principal regulated activity.

enzymes.name: Dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (quinone), mitochondrial
enzymes.description: FMN-dependent flavoenzyme anchored in the inner mitochondrial membrane; oxidizes dihydroorotate and reduces ubiquinone, coupling pyrimidine synthesis to the respiratory chain. Target of leflunomide/teriflunomide and brequinar.

enzymes.name: Uridine 5'-monophosphate synthase (orotate phosphoribosyltransferase · OMP decarboxylase)
enzymes.description: Bifunctional enzyme catalyzing the last two steps to UMP; loss of function causes hereditary orotic aciduria.

enzymes.name: UMP-CMP kinase
enzymes.description: Phosphorylates UMP (and CMP/dCMP) to the diphosphate using ATP.

enzymes.name: Nucleoside diphosphate kinase A
enzymes.description: Broad-specificity NDP kinase; transfers the gamma-phosphate of ATP to UDP giving UTP.

enzymes.name: CTP synthase 1
enzymes.description: Glutamine-dependent amidotransferase that aminates UTP at C4 to CTP; activated by GTP, product-inhibited by CTP, and can polymerize into filaments (cytoophidia). Essential for T- and B-lymphocyte proliferation.

enzymes.name: Mitogen-activated protein kinase 1 (ERK2)
enzymes.description: Regulatory kinase (not a pathway catalyst): growth-factor-activated ERK1/2 phosphorylates CAD, up-regulating CPS II during proliferation.

enzymes.name: cAMP-dependent protein kinase catalytic subunit alpha (PKA C-alpha)
enzymes.description: Regulatory kinase (not a pathway catalyst): PKA phosphorylates CAD and modulates its allosteric responsiveness.

enzymes.name: Glycogen synthase kinase-3 beta (GSK-3 beta)
enzymes.description: Regulatory kinase (not a pathway catalyst): phosphorylates CTP synthase, dampening its synthase/glutaminase activity.

reactions.name: Carbamoyl phosphate synthesis (CPS II, committed step)
reactions.equation: L-Glutamine + 2 ATP + HCO3- + H2O -> Carbamoyl phosphate + L-Glutamate + 2 ADP + Pi + 2 H+

reactions.name: Aspartate transcarbamoylation
reactions.equation: Carbamoyl phosphate + L-Aspartate -> N-Carbamoyl-L-aspartate + Pi + H+

cofactors.role: electron acceptor (respiratory chain); enzyme carries FMN

reactions.name: CTP synthesis (UTP amination)
reactions.equation: UTP + L-Glutamine + ATP + H2O -> CTP + L-Glutamate + ADP + Pi + 2 H+

regulations.mechanism: UTP, the pathway end product, feedback-inhibits the CPS II domain of CAD by stabilizing a low-affinity state for Mg2+/ATP; the dominant control of mammalian de novo pyrimidine synthesis.
PRPP allosterically activates CPS II (raising affinity for Mg2+/ATP), coupling ring synthesis to ribose-phosphate availability.
ATP activates CPS II, matching pyrimidine synthesis to a high cellular energy charge.

Growth-factor-activated MAPK (ERK1/2) phosphorylates CAD, increasing sensitivity to the activator PRPP and decreasing sensitivity to UTP feedback inhibition — up-regulating pyrimidine synthesis in proliferating cells.

PKA phosphorylation of CAD (Ser1406) reduces its responsiveness to allosteric effectors and opposes MAPK activation as cells become quiescent/confluent; note an older hamster study reported PKA activation, so the net direction is context-dependent.
CTP product feedback-inhibits CTP synthase, balancing the CTP pool against UTP; CTPS1 is comparatively weakly inhibited, favoring nucleotide-pool expansion in dividing cells.
GTP allosterically activates the glutamine-dependent amidotransferase (glutaminase) activity of CTP synthase, linking pyrimidine and purine nucleotide supply.
GSK-3 beta phosphorylates CTPS1 at serine residues, inhibiting both its synthase and glutaminase activities.

relations.note: PRPP, the activated ribose-5-phosphate donor used at step 5 and the key CPS II activator, is generated by PRPP synthetase from ribose-5-phosphate of the pentose phosphate pathway.
Aspartate, which contributes N-1, C-4, C-5 and C-6 of the pyrimidine ring, is supplied by transamination of TCA-cycle oxaloacetate.
Carbamoyl phosphate is shared with the urea cycle, but the pools are separate: cytosolic CPS II (CAD) uses glutamine for pyrimidines, whereas mitochondrial CPS I uses ammonia for the urea cycle.
PRPP is the shared committed precursor of both purine and pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis, and GTP activation of CTP synthase further coordinates the two branches.

DHODH reduces ubiquinone to ubiquinol, injecting electrons into the respiratory chain at Complex III; this couples pyrimidine synthesis to mitochondrial respiration and is exploited by DHODH-inhibitor drugs (teriflunomide, brequinar).
UMP is also generated by the salvage pathway from uracil/uridine, which spares de novo flux and shares downstream kinases.
UTP activates glucose-1-phosphate to UDP-glucose, the glucosyl donor for glycogen and glycoconjugate synthesis.
CTP provides cytidylyl groups for CDP-diacylglycerol and CDP-choline/ethanolamine in membrane phospholipid synthesis.
